details: Add 'set surround' to your .ideavimrc
ysiw) wraps a word with parens
cs"' swaps quote types
ds" removes surrounding quotes
Add 'set commentary' to your .ideavimrc
gcc toggles a line comment
gc works with any motion or Visual selection
Add 'set highlightedyank' to your .ideavimrc
The yanked region briefly flashes after every y or Y
Gives visual confirmation of what was copied
Add 'set ReplaceWithRegister' to your .ideavimrc
gr{motion} replaces the motion text with the current register
griw replaces the word without clobbering the yank
Add 'set exchange' to your .ideavimrc
cxiw marks the first region
Repeating on a second region swaps them
Add 'set argtextobj' to your .ideavimrc
ia and aa become text objects for function arguments
cia changes one argument, daa deletes it with its comma
Add 'set textobj-indent' to your .ideavimrc
ii and ai become text objects for the current indent block
vai selects the block, dii deletes it
Add 'set switch' to your .ideavimrc
gs flips paired values like true/false or &&/||
Works on the word under the cursor
Add 'set sneak' to your .ideavimrc
s{cc} jumps forward to the next two-char match
S{cc} searches backward
Add 'set matchit' to your .ideavimrc
% then also jumps between tag pairs and control blocks
Works in HTML, XML, Ruby, and similar languages
Add 'set vim-paragraph-motion' to your .ideavimrc
{ and } treat whitespace-only lines as paragraph breaks
Makes { and } behave like standard Vim on all file types
Add 'set textobj-entire' to your .ideavimrc
ae and ie become text objects for the entire buffer
vae selects everything, yie yanks it
Add 'set NERDTree' to your .ideavimrc
:NERDTreeToggle opens and closes the project tree
:NERDTreeFind reveals the current file in the tree
